error.signature: GCP Pub/Sub delivers duplicate messages despite 'exactly-once delivery' being enabled

verdict.summary: GCP Pub/Sub 'exactly-once delivery' only guarantees exactly-once within the ack deadline. If processing takes longer than ack deadline (default 10s), the message is redelivered. Also, exactly-once is only supported on pull subscriptions.

dead_ends.action: Enable exactly-once delivery and assume no duplicates
dead_ends.why_fails: Exactly-once only works within the ack deadline window. Slow consumers WILL see duplicates. Push subscriptions don't support it at all.

dead_ends.action: Increase ack deadline to maximum (600s) to avoid redelivery
dead_ends.why_fails: 600s max may still not be enough for slow processing. Also, increases message invisibility window on failure.
If processing fails after ack, the message is lost forever. No redelivery for acknowledged messages.

workarounds.action: Implement idempotent message processing using message_id
Use modack to extend deadline during long processing
workarounds.how: Periodically call modify_ack_deadline() in a background thread while processing

workarounds.action: Design consumers to be idempotent regardless of delivery guarantees
workarounds.how: Use database upserts, idempotency keys, or dedup tables. Never assume exactly-once from any message queue.
